Off Calendar Oct-21-2005 Continued To Oct-31-05 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel further responses to request for production set #1:
The Court has considered the late filed opposition by plaintiff. On the Court's own motion, the hearing is continued to October 31, 2005 at 9:00 a.m. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to the sufficiency of the supplemental responses and production. If a further hearing is necessary, plaintiff and defendant are ordered respectively to submit a substantive opposition and a supplemental separate statement of matters in dispute by 4:00 p.m. October 27, 2005. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN

Motion To Compel Supplemental Responses To Capital One'S First Set Of Form Interrogatories And For Imposition Of Sanctions; Rule 335 Statement In Support Of Motion To Compel
Off Calendar Oct-21-2005 Continued To Oct-31-05 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. Defendant's motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories set #1:
The Court has considered the late filed opposition by plaintiff. On the Court's own motion, the hearing is continued to October 31, 2005 at 9:00 a.m. The parties are ordered to meet and confer as to the sufficiency of the supplemental responses. If a further hearing is necessary, plaintiff and defendant are ordered respectively to submit a substantive opposition and a supplemental separate statement of matters in dispute by 4:00 p.m. October 27, 2005. The Court has reviewed the supplemental responses served on October 11, 2005 and notes that the preliminary statement should be stricken. The Code of Civil Procedure does not provide for general objections. The Court also notes that interrogatories 8.4, 8.7, and 8.8 do not request information protected by either the attorney work product or attorney client privilege. The objection based on the right of privacy would be overruled. Additionally, the questions do not seek an expert opinion or otherwise contravene the statutory scheme set forth in CCP Section 2034 et seq. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
